title: SACRAMENTO

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 1927 provides immunity for victims and witnesses of sexual assault from the consequences of illegal alcohol or drug use occurring at the time of their alleged assault.",
    "Higher education institutions nationwide, including the University of California, have implemented amnesty clauses on their campuses.",
    "Victims of sexual assault who were under the influence of alcohol and drugs during the abuse are more reluctant to testify against their assailants for fear of personal prosecution.",
    "AB 1927 will provide assault survivors with a safe space to provide testimony before a judge without incriminating them for their behavior.",
    "Current law requires victims to be compelled to testify in court before amnesty for alcohol or drug charges can be granted.",
    "The process of compelling victims to testify unnecessarily increases trauma to the victim by requiring them to appear in front of a judge for offenses unrelated to the sexual assault.",
    "AB 1927 remedies the underreporting of sexual assault cases by creating a statutory vehicle to provide victims and witnesses relief from potential criminal liability for self-incriminating testimony.",
    "AB 1927 limits liability for drug-related or alcohol crimes to situations where the victim or witness testified in a sexual assault prosecution and the testimony was incriminating.",
    "AB 1927 does not prohibit the accused from introducing evidence that the accuser was under the influence."
  ]
}
```
